commands.review_summary:
The PR retunes the custom Refresh menu row metrics, lets its SF Symbol weight come from the metrics, changes the Usage Dashboard system image, and updates focused menu tests.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a bug reproduction path; this is a visual polish PR. The PR supplies before/after screenshots and current main source confirms the older metrics and dashboard icon are still present, but I did not run a live packaged-app reproduction.

target.title: Stabilize refresh row alignment across provider menus
target.body: ## Summary - Tune the custom Refresh row metrics to better match native AppKit menu rows. - Use a smaller semibold Refresh symbol to match neighboring menu icons. - Change the Usage Dashboard icon to `chart.xyaxis.line`. ## Reasoning Follow-up to [#1729](https://github.com/steipete/CodexBar/pull/1729). The Refresh row stays custom because clicking it or pressing `⌘R` should refresh while keeping the menu open. A native key equivalent would let AppKit handle the shortcut and dismiss the menu. The earlier fix used one fixed Refresh inset based on the menus measured then. Later testing showed there are two native layout cases: menus with the Usage Dashboard row, and menus like Overview/Antigravity that may not have it. AppKit can place native rows slightly differently between those cases, so changing only the Refresh padding is not enough. The previous Dashboard icon was wide enough to affect AppKit’s native image/text columns. Switching it to the narrower `chart.xyaxis.line` avoids triggering that native row drift, while still fitting the dashboard action. With the native rows stabilized, the Refresh row metrics are tuned to the final visually tested values. ## Testing - `swift test --filter StatusMenuPersistentRefreshTests` passed. - `make test` - `make check` ## Screenshots Bef ore: After:
